body:value: The data refers to forest cover in North-Eastern States. Forest Cover refers to all lands more than one hectare in area, with a tree canopy density of more than 10 percent irrespective of ownership and legal status. Such lands may not necessarily be a recorded forest area. It also includes orchards, bamboo and palm.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Environment and Forest
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Environment and Forest
title: Forest Cover in the North-Eastern States

body:value: The data refers to forest cover in Tribal districts in India. Forest Cover refers to all lands more than one hectare in area, with a tree canopy density of more than 10 percent irrespective of ownership and legal status. Such lands may not necessarily be a recorded forest area. It also includes orchards, bamboo and palm.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Environment and Forest
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog

body:value: The data refers to time series of forest cover of India showing the transformation with respect to methodology changes for making the area figures comparable. It provides details of forest cover (as reported in SFR), smoothened forest cover (using moving average of three), forest cover corrected for change in scale, forest cover corrected for change in scale and inclusion of small patches and forest cover corrected for change in scale, inclusion of small patches and vector approach.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Environment and Forest
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Environment and Forest
title: Forest Cover of India - Methodology Changes

body:value:
Get detailed profile of former members of Rajya Sabha such as name, state, party, address details and their biographical details.
The Rajya Sabha or Council of States is the upper house of the Parliament of India. Members sit for six-year terms, with one third of the members retiring every two years.The Rajya Sabha meets in continuous sessions and, unlike the Lok Sabha, the lower house of Parliament, is not subject to dissolution.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Rajya Sabha
field_ministry_department:name: Rajya Sabha
field_sector:name: Parliament Of india
Governance and Administration
Rajya Sabha
title: Former Members of Rajya Sabha in English

body:value: The data refers to Frequency of Fruits Intake of beneficiary children at home. It provide details on the frequency of fruits intake (daily, sometimes in a week, sometimes in month/year and never) in percentage terms for the selected sample states. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission which covered 48 districts and 480 schools across 17 states of India for 'Performance Evaluation Report of Cooked Mid-Day Meal (CMDM), 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2000 to 2006.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Frequency of Fruits Intake of beneficiary children at home - Based on Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to details of Frequency of meetings held by VECs (in %) for the surveyed states. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission in eleven states and two union territories for 'Evaluation Report On Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan, 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2003 to 2007.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Frequency of meetings held by VECs - Based on Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to Frequency of Meetings of Block level Monitoring Teams. It provides details of number of blocks, number of block monitoring teams, frequency of visits of block monitoring teams to schools (weekly, fortnightly, monthly, half yearly, yearly) and participation of NGOs in monitoring. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission in eleven states and two union territories for 'Evaluation Report On Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan, 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2003 to 2007.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Frequency of Meetings of Block level Monitoring Teams - Based on Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to Frequency of Milk Intake of beneficiary children at home. It provide details on the frequency of milk intake (daily, sometimes in a week, sometimes in month/year and never) in percentage terms for the selected sample states. About 75 percent of the selected children in Andhra Pradesh said they do not get milk at home. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission which covered 48 districts and 480 schools across 17 states of India for 'Performance Evaluation Report of Cooked Mid-Day Meal (CMDM), 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2000 to 2006.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Frequency of Milk Intake of beneficiary children at home - Based on Sample Survey
